title: Upgraded Luggage Rack / Suitcase Stand Bench
summary: A small X-leg bench with a slatted shelf, sized as a suitcase stand — the notched, interlocking legs are the whole design.
description:
The entire bench comes down to one joint done right: each pair of 2x2 legs is cut at a 35° compound angle, then one leg in every pair gets notched so the two cross and interlock, rather than simply overlapping and getting screwed through. Cutting the notch is genuinely fussy — a series of shallow saw kerfs every 1/4" across the overlap, then a chisel to clear the waste between them — but it's what lets the X sit flush and take a screw straight through the center without one leg standing proud of the other.

Every leg gets notched as a mirror image of its pair, which is why the plan insists on cutting all four to the same compound angle before doing any notching: two mirrored legs make one clean X, and a leg cut to the wrong hand ruins the fit no amount of chiseling can fix.

tools.note: Cuts the series of kerfs for notching the legs.
Clears the waste between the notch kerfs.

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 35° off square, long point to short point, ends parallel; notch each leg to mirror its pair.
Cut three from each of two 8 ft boards.
Cut two from each remaining board length.

steps.title: Cut and notch the legs
steps.body: Using a compound miter saw, cut the legs at 35° off square, both ends parallel to each other, measuring long point to short point. Set a pair of legs as they'll cross and mark the overlap. Notch out the overlap with the circular saw set to 3/4" depth, making a series of cuts every 1/4" across the marked area, then clear the waste with a chisel. Notch each leg as a mirror image of its pair, so two legs together form a clean X.

steps.title: Assemble the leg sets
steps.body: Attach the top crosspiece to each leg set with 2-1/2" wood screws. Add a brad nail or staple and glue at the overlapping center of each X for extra hold.

steps.title: Build the bench frame
steps.body: Attach the frame sides to the leg sets with glue and 1-1/4" brad nails to form the bench frame.

steps.title: Attach the shelf boards
steps.body: Place the shelf boards on the frame sides with equal overhang and about 1" spacing between boards — cutting a 1" spacer block makes this quick and consistent. Nail at an angle through the boards into the frame with glue and 1-1/4" brads or staples.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand every surface smooth and apply your finish of choice.

images.alt: Small X-leg luggage rack bench with a slatted shelf
